zh.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es是William de Es的兒子，出生。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1205年。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es是William de Es的儿子，出生。他至少有1个儿子。他去世于1205年。

nameMeanings.name: Es
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places so named.
English: variant of Emm , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. See Imm .

nameMeanings.name: Eustace
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old French form of the Late Greek names Eustakhios and Eustathios. These were evidently of separate origin, the former derived from eu ‘well, good’ + stakhys ‘grapes’, the latter from eu + stēnai ‘to stand’. However, the tradition is very confused. The name was introduced in this form to Britain by the Normans, among whom it was popular as a result of the fame of St Eustace , a Norman nobleman who was said to have been converted to Christianity by the vision of a crucifix between the antlers of the stag he was hunting.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es was born as the son of William de Es. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1205.

fr.lifeSummary: Eustace d’Es est né, fils de William d’Es. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1205.

de.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es wurde, als Sohn von William de Es geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1205.

it.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es è nato il come figlio di William de Es. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1205.

ja.lifeSummary: Eustace de EsはWilliam de Esの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1205年に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es(은)는 William de Es의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1205년 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es nasceu filho de William de Es. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1205.

ru.lifeSummary: Eustace de Es родился. Его отца звали William de Es. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1205 г.
